meta.title: Chao turns 40
meta.blurb: Part reunion, part food coma, part shameless excuse to fly Business. 17 friends, 6 countries, one birthday that's going to be very hard to top.
meta.invite:
Hey, it's finally happening — I'm turning 40.

This trip isn't just about celebrating my birthday. It's about celebrating some of the best bits of my life, especially the incredible humans I've collected along the way. That means you.

You've all been part of my story in different ways, across different chapters, countries, continents, adventures, and mildly questionable decisions. So I thought: what better way to mark 40 than by bringing some of my favourite people together for one big trip across Hong Kong, Shenzhen, and Taiwan?

We'll explore new places, eat unknown foods, get a bit lost, laugh a lot, and create the kind of memories we'll still be talking about years from now.

Here's to 40, friendship, food, adventure, and just the right amount of chaos.
meta.playful: 17 friends · 3 cities · 1 very full camera roll

destinations.name: Hong Kong
destinations.blurb: Where the whole crew lands and the birthday kicks off.
destinations.why: If you had to pick one city that is basically Chao in physical form, it's Hong Kong. East meets West, chaos meets order, dim sum at any hour and a skyline that doesn't quit. It's loud, it's alive, and it doesn't apologise for either. So when it came to picking where 17 people from 6 countries would fly halfway across the world to watch one man get old, this was always the answer. No debate needed.

destinations.name: Shenzhen
destinations.blurb: A quick high-speed-rail hop north for a night.
destinations.why: Honestly we just want to go and see some cool stuff. Robots walking the streets, drones in the sky, lights everywhere. Shenzhen is the city of 2050 and we're going to experience it firsthand. Someone might even bring a robot home.

destinations.name: Taipei
destinations.blurb: Night markets, Jiufen, and Taipei Pride to finish.
destinations.why: Asia's best-kept secret and somehow still underrated. A melting pot where Japanese precision, Chinese soul, and Southeast Asian warmth all collide in the best possible way. Closest to where Chao was born, built around food you'll be dreaming about for weeks, and the kind of chill you simply can't manufacture.

days.summary: Wheels down in Taipei. Last city. Best city?

plans.plan: Check in to the Meihua Village Airbnb (keypad self check-in).
First night in Taipei · plans TBC.

days.title: Taipei: open day
days.summary: No agenda. Just Taipei, your feet, and an embarrassing amount of food.

days.title: Jiufen: the Ghibli town
days.summary: Lantern alleys, tea houses, golden hour.

plans.plan: ☀️ Early start → Jiufen Old Street. Walk to Shandao Temple MRT → Taipei Main Station → train (TRA) to Ruifang, then bus/taxi up. Arrive before the crowds; tea + breakfast snacks.
🏮 Explore Jiufen: A-Mei Tea House viewpoint, lantern alleys, shops. Street-food crawl: taro balls, peanut ice-cream roll, fish balls, herbal tea. Optional: Jinguashi Gold Museum / Golden Waterfall.
🌇 Stay for golden hour + lanterns, then bus/taxi → Ruifang → train back. Dinner near Dongmen / Yongkang St or Zhongxiao Fuxing.

days.title: Taipei: open day
days.summary: Last free day before the main event. Make it count.

days.title: Taipei Pride
days.summary: Asia's biggest Pride. The grand finale.

days.title: Last day in Taipei
days.summary: Bags by the door. One last bowl of something good. Then the airport.

plans.plan: 🧳 Check out of the Airbnb by 11:00.
Head to Taoyuan Airport (TPE) · last Taipei shopping / bites.
✈️ CX531: Taipei 8:15pm → Hong Kong 10:20pm · overnight in HK.

days.summary: Red-eye home. Until the next one.
